articles.title: Redick Wants Lakers’ Core Trio Back Next Season
articles.original_title: JJ Redick Wants Core Trio Of Luka Doncic, Austin Reaves & LeBron James Back Next Season
articles.summary: Former Laker JJ Redick said the combination of Luka Doncic, Austin Reaves and LeBron James showed flashes of elite chemistry after early‑season growing pains. He believes that once the trio clicks, they could be among the NBA’s best units, and he hopes the team retains all three for the 2025‑26 campaign.
key_points: Redick praised the on‑court synergy of Doncic, Reaves and James after the season’s rough start.
He emphasized that the trio’s potential makes them a cornerstone for future success.
Redick urged the organization to keep the three players together for next year.

articles.title: Lakers ‘Love’ Having LeBron James Return Next Season
articles.original_title: Lakers would ‘love’ to have LeBron James back next season
articles.summary: LeBron James will enter unrestricted free agency for the first time since joining Los Angeles in 2018, leaving his future uncertain. The Lakers publicly expressed a strong desire to retain him, while acknowledging that all options—including a move elsewhere—remain on the table.
key_points: James becomes an unrestricted free agent, ending his contract with the Lakers.
The organization stated it would “love” to keep him for the 2025‑26 season.
Potential destinations include staying in LA, returning to Cleveland, or joining another contender.

articles.title: LeBron James Favored to Return, Yet May Leave Lakers – Reuters
articles.original_title: LeBron James favored to return next season, but leave Lakers - Reuters
articles.summary: Reuters reports that while LeBron James is the favorite to re‑sign with the Los Angeles Lakers, there is also a realistic possibility he could depart in free agency. The article highlights the uncertainty surrounding his decision and its impact on the franchise’s planning.
key_points: James is the leading candidate to stay with the Lakers for another year.
Analysts note a credible chance he could sign elsewhere, creating roster uncertainty.
The Lakers must prepare for both scenarios as they approach the offseason.

articles.title: Doncic ‘Wasn’t Close’ to Returning for Lakers Playoffs
articles.original_title: Lakers News: Luka Doncic ‘Wasn’t Close’ To Returning In Playoffs
articles.summary: The Lakers’ 2025‑26 season ended with a second‑round sweep by the Oklahoma City Thunder, a respectable run given Luka Doncic’s Grade‑2 left hamstring strain that sidelined him on April 2. Despite his absence, the team reached the playoffs, but Doncic confirmed he was far from being able to play in the series.
key_points: Luka Doncic suffered a Grade‑2 left hamstring strain, ending his season on April 2.
The Lakers still advanced to the second round, ultimately being swept by OKC.
Doncic stated he was not close to returning for the postseason.

articles.title: Game 4 Exposes Urgent Offseason Need for Lakers
articles.original_title: How Game 4 revealed one urgent need for the Lakers this offseason
articles.summary: In Game 4 of the Western Conference semifinals, the Oklahoma City Thunder completed a sweep of the Los Angeles Lakers, highlighting a critical deficiency in the Lakers’ roster. The loss, despite a brief late‑game lead, underscored the urgency for the team to address specific weaknesses before the next season.
key_points: Thunder closed the series with a 4‑0 sweep, ending the Lakers’ playoff run.
Lakers showed brief resilience but could not hold a one‑point lead in the final minute.
The defeat revealed a pressing need for roster upgrades in the upcoming offseason.

articles.title: Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander lets OKC shine, then caps win over Lakers
articles.original_title: Sha Gilgeous-Alexander let OKC Thunder teammates shine, then he closed out Lakers - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander paced the Oklahoma City Thunder, sharing the ball with teammates throughout the game before delivering the decisive play that sealed a victory against the Los Angeles Lakers. The performance highlighted his ability to both facilitate and finish in high‑stakes moments.
key_points: Gilgeous‑Alexander distributed the offense early, allowing multiple Thunder players to contribute.
He scored the game‑closing basket that gave OKC a win over the Lakers.
The outing underscored his dual role as a playmaker and clutch scorer.

articles.title: Luka Dončić tells Lakers he wants to stay with Austin Reaves
articles.original_title: Luka Dončić ‘made it clear’ to Lakers he wants to continue playing with Austin Reaves
articles.summary: Luka Dončić communicated to the Los Angeles Lakers that he wishes to continue his on‑court partnership with Austin Reaves, a duo that emerged as one of the league’s most effective backcourts this season. Their chemistry helped Reaves elevate to All‑Star‑caliber play.
key_points: Dončić explicitly expressed a desire to keep playing alongside Reaves.
The Reaves‑Dončić pairing became a top backcourt combination in the NBA.
Reaves’ performance rose to near All‑Star level during their collaboration.

articles.title: Rob Pelinka proud but unsatisfied after Lakers’ 2025‑26 season sweep
articles.original_title: Rob Pelinka Proud But Not Satisfied With Lakers’ 2025-26 Season
articles.summary: General manager Rob Pelinka reflected on the Lakers’ 2025‑26 campaign, which ended with a sweep by the Oklahoma City Thunder. While he praised the roster built around Luka Dončić as his best since the championship core, Pelinka acknowledged the season fell short of expectations.
key_points: Lakers were eliminated by the Thunder in a predictable sweep.
Pelinka considers the roster constructed around Dončić his strongest since the championship era.
Despite pride in the roster, Pelinka remains dissatisfied with the overall outcome.

articles.title: Pelinka plans to add two assistant GMs for scouting and salary‑cap strategy
articles.original_title: [Murray] Rob Pelinka says that Lakers will hire two assistant general managers: - one for pro scouting/draft/evaluation - one for strategy/salary cap
articles.summary: Rob Pelinka announced that the Lakers will hire two assistant general managers: one focused on pro scouting, draft evaluation, and player assessment, and another dedicated to strategic planning and salary‑cap management. The move aims to address past missteps in player targeting while building on Pelinka’s negotiation strengths.
key_points: Two new assistant GM roles will be created – scouting/draft and strategy/cap management.
Pelinka’s negotiation successes (e.g., Luka trade) are highlighted as strengths.
The hires are intended to improve player acquisition and overall roster strategy.

articles.title: Lakers and Austin Reaves express mutual interest in continuing partnership
articles.original_title: Lakers & Austin Reaves Have Mutual Interest In Continuing Partnership
articles.summary: After five seasons with the Los Angeles Lakers, Austin Reaves, the longest‑tenured player after LeBron James, has emerged from an undrafted two‑way contract to a borderline All‑Star status. Both Reaves and the organization have indicated a desire to keep their partnership intact moving forward.
key_points: Reaves progressed from undrafted two‑way player to near All‑Star level.
He is the longest‑tenured Laker after LeBron James, now in his fifth season.
Both the player and the franchise have shown interest in extending the relationship.

articles.title: Brandon Clarke's Death Under Investigation
articles.original_title: What happened to Brandon Clarke? Authorities investigating NBA player's death
articles.summary: Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke was found dead at age 29. Authorities are conducting an investigation to determine the cause of death, and the NBA has confirmed the tragedy.
key_points: Clarke, 29, passed away unexpectedly.
The Grizzlies and NBA have announced his death.
Law enforcement is investigating the circumstances.

articles.title: Steve Kerr Signs Two‑Year Extension with Warriors
articles.original_title: Steve Kerr signs new two-year contract to continue coaching the Golden State Warriors
articles.summary: Golden State Warriors head coach Steve Kerr agreed to a new two‑year contract, keeping him behind the bench through the 2025‑26 season. Kerr expressed gratitude for the opportunity to continue leading the team.
key_points: Kerr's contract extension was announced Tuesday.
The deal covers the next two NBA seasons.
Kerr thanked the organization in a public statement.

articles.title: Timberwolves vs. Spurs Game 5 Preview and Picks
articles.original_title: Timberwolves vs Spurs Prediction, Picks & Odds for Tonight's NBA Playoffs Game 5
articles.summary: Analysts highlight Victor Wembanyama's dominant rebounding as a key factor in the upcoming Game 5 of the NBA playoffs between Minnesota and San Antonio. Predictions and betting odds are provided for the matchup.
key_points: Wembanyama is noted for his glass‑cleaning performance.
The article offers game predictions and betting lines.
Game 5 decides which team advances in the playoff series.

articles.title: NBA Confirms Brandon Clarke’s Death at 29
articles.original_title: NBA player Clarke dies aged 29
articles.summary: The Memphis Grizzlies and the NBA have confirmed that forward Brandon Clarke died at the age of 29. No cause of death has been released pending an official investigation.
key_points: Clarke’s death was publicly confirmed by the team and league.
He was 29 years old at the time of his passing.
Details regarding the cause remain under investigation.

articles.summary: The death of Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke was announced Tuesday, May 12. Clarke played seven seasons in the NBA, all with Memphis.

articles.title: Pelinka: Lakers Want LeBron Back, Reaves Wants Return, Dončić Gets Roster Say‑So
articles.original_title: Rob Pelinka: Lakers would 'love to have LeBron James' back, Austin Reaves wants to return; Luka Dončić will have roster input - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: General manager Rob Pelinka said the Lakers would love to have LeBron James return, while guard Austin Reaves has expressed a desire to stay with the team. Pelinka also noted that Luka Dončić will have input on roster decisions, indicating a collaborative approach to future moves.
key_points: LeBron James is a top free‑agency target for the Lakers.
Austin Reaves wants to remain with Los Angeles for the next season.
Luka Dončić will be consulted on roster construction and player acquisitions.

articles.title: Dončić Says He Was Close to Re‑Joining Lakers in Playoffs
articles.original_title: Luka Doncic reveals how close he was to returning in playoffs - Lakers Nation
articles.summary: Luka Dončić revealed that he was nearly back with the Los Angeles Lakers for the postseason, indicating that negotiations came close to fruition before the playoffs began. The details of why the deal fell through were not disclosed.
key_points: Dončić was within striking distance of a playoff‑time return to LA.
Negotiations ended before the postseason started.
No specific reasons were given for the collapse of the deal.

articles.title: Dončić Wants to Keep Playing With Austin Reaves, Says Lakers
articles.original_title: Luka Dončić ‘made it clear’ to Lakers he wants to continue playing with Austin Reaves - Silver Screen and Roll
articles.summary: According to Silver Screen and Roll, Luka Dončić made it clear to the Lakers organization that he prefers to continue playing alongside guard Austin Reaves. The statement underscores the chemistry between the two players and could influence future roster moves.
key_points: Dončić expressed a strong desire to stay on the court with Austin Reaves.
The preference may affect the Lakers' offseason planning.
Team chemistry between Dončić and Reaves is highlighted as a priority.

articles.title: LeBron James Enjoys Leading Lakers, Open to Reduced Role
articles.original_title: LeBron James relishes leading Lakers. Would he accept lesser role again? - USA Today
articles.summary: USA Today reports that LeBron James relishes his leadership role with the Los Angeles Lakers and is willing to accept a smaller on‑court role if it benefits the team. The article explores how his mindset could shape roster decisions moving forward.
key_points: James values his leadership and team‑first attitude.
He is open to playing fewer minutes or a reduced role.
His flexibility may influence the Lakers' strategy in the upcoming season.

articles.title: Perkins Suggests Knicks Could Be Viable Landing Spot for LeBron
articles.original_title: Could Knicks emerge as serious landing spot for LeBron James? Kendrick Perkins thinks so - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: Former NBA player Kendrick Perkins speculated that the New York Knicks might emerge as a serious destination for LeBron James, citing the team's recent moves and market appeal. The comment adds another layer to the ongoing free‑agency chatter surrounding James.
key_points: Perkins sees the Knicks as a potential fit for LeBron James.
Recent Knicks roster changes and market size are cited as factors.
The speculation contributes to the broader discussion of James' free‑agency options.
